mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate income expectations, causing investors to mark down future corporate cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5% triggers forced de-risking by institutional investors facing volatility-targeting constraints and redemption pressure, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, positive 20-day returns occur in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a federal funds rate below 1% and declining industrial production exhausts monetary policy buffers while confirming real economic contraction, producing nonlinear negative wealth effects that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with industrial contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 45% of joint-triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-led recessions, month-over-month declines in housing starts transmit through construction employment losses and mortgage credit deterioration to depress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines month-over-month during housing-led recessions, positive 20-day returns occur in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% signals acute financial dislocation that overwhelms the stimulative discount rate channel, causing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as liquidity premiums spike and counterparty risk rises.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment and falling retail sales creates a nonlinear consumption drag that depresses discretionary earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increasing month-over-month and RSAFS declining month-over-month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of months when both UNRATE increases and RSAFS decreases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles with the policy rate below 1%, month-over-month CPI increases above 0.3% signal receding deflationary risk, reducing real debt burdens and generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns via reflationary repricing.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with policy rate below 1%
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises >0.3% MoM while FEDFUNDS is below 1%, 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production trigger immediate inventory destocking fears and cyclical earnings compression, driving negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as markets front-run manufacturing sector profit warnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of months when INDPRO declines month-over-month.
